content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://epub.jku.at/download/pdf/9550275.pdf", "source_title": "Effect of global damp heat ageing on debonding of crosslinked EVA- and POE-glass laminates", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.88, "overall_relevance": 0.9, "year": 2024, "authors": ["Tiefenthaler M", "Wallner GM", "Pugstaller R"], "venue": "Solar Energy Materials and Solar Cells", "doi": "10.1016/j.solmat.2023.112602", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Compressive shear performance of EVA double-glass laminates was not significantly affected by damp heat aging at 85 C/85% RH for up to 10,000 hours, while POE laminates showed even improved compressive shear performance.", "direct_quote": "The compressive shear behavior of EVA laminates was not significantly affected by damp heat ageing. Interestingly, the POE laminates revealed an even improved compressive shear performance. Specimens were damp heat exposed (85 C, 85 %rh) in an environmental chamber for 0, 1,000, 2,000, 5,000 and 10,000h", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.95, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["compressive shear", "EVA", "POE", "85 C", "85% RH", "10000 hours"]}, {"statement": "EVA laminates consistently failed interfacially regardless of DH duration, while POE laminates transitioned from purely interfacial (unaged) to combined interfacial and cohesive failure after 10,000 hours.", "direct_quote": "Whereas the tested EVA laminates failed in an interfacial manner independent on damp heat ageing time, POE laminates exhibited a failure mode transition from interfacial (unaged) to combined interfacial and cohesive (10,000h). On the interfacially fractured surfaces of EVA and POE laminates, carboxylic acid groups were ascertained", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.92, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["interfacial failure", "cohesive failure", "EVA", "POE", "10000 hours"]}, {"statement": "Birefringent spheric microdomains (maltese-crosses) formed in both laminates after 2,000+ hours DH; EVA had fewer but larger domains related to water uptake, POE had more homogenous smaller domains.", "direct_quote": "the formation of birefringent spheric microdomains (maltese-crosses) was confirmed after an exposure time of more than 2,000h. Compared to POE, EVA laminates exhibited less, but larger microdomains, which were presumably related to water uptake and clustering. The microdomains within the POE laminates were smaller and more homogenous", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.82, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["maltese-crosses", "microdomains", "2000 hours", "EVA", "POE"]}, {"statement": "DH exposure of POE for 10,000 hours caused pronounced glass aging near the interface with partly cohesive failure in glass, attributed to the alkaline characteristics of the HALS-stabilized POE.", "direct_quote": "Damp heat exposure of POE laminates for 10,000h resulted in pronounced glass ageing close to the interface associated with a partly cohesive failure within glass. This phenomenon was quite different to EVA and was attributed to the alkaline characteristics of the hindered amine light (HALS) stabilized, less-polar, UV-transparent POE encapsulant", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.89, "perspective": "Emerging_Trends", "entities": ["POE", "glass aging", "cohesive failure", "HALS", "alkaline", "10000 hours"]}, {"statement": "XPS detected carboxylic acid groups on fractured encapsulant surfaces and deprotonated silanol and water bands on glass sides, indicating hydrolytic degradation at the encapsulant-glass interface.", "direct_quote": "On the interfacially fractured surfaces of EVA and POE laminates, carboxylic acid groups were ascertained. Deprotonated silanol and water bands were discernible on the opposite glass sides. Compressive shear tests were carried out in tensile mode on an universal tensile testing machine at 60 C to mimic critical service-relevant conditions", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["XPS", "carboxylic acid", "silanol", "hydrolytic degradation"]}, {"statement": "POE laminates showed 5.8% drop in solar hemispheric transmittance after 10,000h DH and 19.0% UV transmittance loss for both EVA and POE, while EVA solar haze increased 9x after 1,000 hours.", "direct_quote": "A pronounced drop in solar transmittance (by -5.8%) was ascertained just for the POE laminate. Damp heat exposure for 10,000h caused a significant decrease (-19.0%) of the UV transmittance for both, EVA and POE laminates. The solar haze increased by a factor of 9 after already 1,000h of damp heat exposure of EVA laminates", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.72, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["transmittance", "-5.8%", "-19.0%", "solar haze", "9x", "10000 hours"]}, {"statement": "Higher polarity of EVA favors ion diffusion into the bulk, and DH-induced hydrolysis forms acetic acid which keeps EVA-glass interface pH below 9, hindering sodium or calcium carbonate formation.", "direct_quote": "the higher polarity of EVA favors ion diffusion into the bulk of the encapsulant. Moreover, the damp heat induced hydrolysis of EVA could facilitate the formation of acetic acid, which would reduce the alkalinity of the pH at the EVA-glass interface.
